name: KB Home

profile.description: KB Home operates as a homebuilding company in the United States. It builds and sells homes, including attached and detached single-family residential homes, townhomes, and condominiums primarily for first-time, first move-up, and active adult homebuyers.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: KB Home has invested in digital transformation under CIO Greg Moore, integrating AI tools into the home-buying process for personalized customer experiences and operational streamlining [2]. The company's built-to-order model with a design studio concept differentiates it from spec builders, but homebuilding remains a fundamentally low-innovation industry. KB Home was the first national builder to adopt EPA WaterSense standards and has 14 consecutive ENERGY STAR Partner of the Year awards, reflecting process innovation in sustainability [3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: CEO Rob McGibney took the helm in 2024, providing fresh leadership after Jeff Mezger's long tenure, but it is early to assess cultural transformation [2]. Comparably leadership ratings are mixed — C- from some employees but Top 15% executive team rating — suggesting inconsistent inspiration across the organization [2]. The company's mission of making homeownership accessible provides a meaningful purpose floor, particularly given its focus on first-time homebuyers.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Q1 FY2026 revenue declined 22.6% YoY to $1.08B with EPS of just $0.52, reflecting severe housing market headwinds from elevated mortgage rates [1]. Profit margin compressed to 5.9% and ROE fell to 8.9%, well below historical norms for the builder [1]. The P/E of 9.9x and forward P/E of 11.4x reflect market skepticism, though D/E of 49.8% is conservative for homebuilders. Full-year guidance of $5.1-$6.1B revenue suggests management expects a back-half recovery [1].

ai_resilience.label: NEUTRAL
ai_resilience.analysis: AI poses minimal direct disruption risk to homebuilding — houses still need to be physically constructed — but AI offers moderate efficiency gains in design, customer matching, supply chain optimization, and sales processes [2]. KB Home's CIO Greg Moore is actively integrating AI into the home-buying experience and operations, positioning the company to capture productivity improvements [2]. However, AI will not fundamentally transform the cyclical nature of the housing market or solve interest rate sensitivity.

infinite_game.summary: KB Home demonstrates commitment to long-term sustainability leadership (16 consecutive sustainability reports) and has maintained its built-to-order model through multiple cycles, but the company's heavy dependence on housing cycles creates inherent short-term pressures that challenge infinite game play [3].

just_cause.analysis: Making homeownership accessible and affordable — particularly for first-time buyers — is a genuine just cause with societal impact [3]. KB Home's sustainability leadership (14 consecutive ENERGY STAR awards, first builder in WaterSense program) demonstrates commitment beyond profit to environmental stewardship in the building industry [3].

trusting_teams.analysis: Mixed signals from employee reviews — Comparably shows C- leadership rating alongside Top 15% executive team score, suggesting trust varies significantly by level and division [2]. The CEO transition in 2024 may be creating uncertainty among some employees during the cultural shift from Mezger's long tenure.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Competes against larger national builders (D.R. Horton, Lennar, NVR) who benefit from greater scale and spec-build efficiency. KB Home's differentiation through built-to-order customization acknowledges where rivals have scale advantages while competing on personalization and energy efficiency.

existential_flexibility.analysis: KB Home has adapted through multiple housing cycles and shifted toward sustainability as a competitive differentiator, but the core business model of building single-family homes in select U.S. markets remains largely unchanged over decades. Geographic concentration in California, Texas, Arizona, and Nevada limits diversification.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Early adoption of sustainability standards (WaterSense in 2010, ENERGY STAR leadership) showed courage to invest ahead of regulatory requirements [3]. However, the company has not made transformative moves to diversify beyond traditional homebuilding during favorable market conditions.

investment_thesis: KB Home is a well-managed homebuilder with industry-leading sustainability credentials and a differentiated built-to-order model, but is currently facing severe cyclical headwinds from elevated mortgage rates that have compressed margins and driven a 65% EPS decline in Q1 FY2026. The conservative balance sheet (D/E 49.8%) and low valuation (9.9x trailing P/E) provide downside protection.
thesis_continued: The company's guidance of $5.1-$6.1B in FY2026 revenue implies management expects a second-half recovery, potentially driven by rate cuts or seasonal demand improvement. At current prices, KBH is a cyclical value play that requires conviction on housing recovery timing — the wide guidance range ($5.1-$6.1B) reflects genuine uncertainty about demand trajectory.
key_catalysts: Mortgage rate declines from Fed easing would directly boost demand and order activity for first-time homebuyers
Back-half FY2026 recovery with stronger regional mix and operating leverage from higher delivery volumes per management guidance
Continued sustainability leadership differentiating KB Home in markets with increasingly strict building codes and buyer preferences
key_risks: Prolonged elevated mortgage rates could extend the housing downturn beyond management's back-half recovery expectation, compressing margins further
Revenue decline of 22.6% in Q1 FY2026 with EPS down 65% may signal structural demand weakness rather than temporary cyclical softness
Geographic concentration in high-cost markets (California, Arizona, Nevada) amplifies sensitivity to affordability constraints
sources: KB Home Q1 FY2026 Earnings Release, March 2026
Comparably Culture Ratings and CIO Greg Moore Interview, Metis Strategy, accessed May 2026
HousingWire — KB Home 16th Sustainability Report and EPA ENERGY STAR Awards, accessed May 2026
Yahoo Finance — KBH key statistics, accessed May 2026
